sourceMovie.title: Johnny English
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Johnny English (2003) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Peter Howitt's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spies in Disguise
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Johnny English clicked for you, Spies in Disguise (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Skyfall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Johnny English clicked for you, Skyfall (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Johnny English?
faq.answer: Try Spies in Disguise, Skyfall, Quantum of Solace—each shares DNA with Johnny English while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Johnny English?
faq.answer: Johnny English (2003) is best described as Adventure, Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Johnny English part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Johnny English Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
